message:
refactor(runtime): Apply pyAnyEql consistently across codebase

Replace std.meta.eql with runtime.pyAnyEql throughout the codebase
to ensure Python-semantic comparison is used everywhere.

## Changes

### comparison.zig (codegen)
- Always emit runtime.pyAnyEql for non-primitive comparisons
- Previously used std.meta.eql which fails for NaN comparisons
- This ensures `nan == nan` in containers works correctly

### assertions_basic.zig (unittest)
- Replace pythonEql and elemEql with runtime.pyAnyEql
- Update equalArrayList, equalHashMap to use pyAnyEql
- Update equalValues, assertNotEqual, assertIs to use pyAnyEql
- Removed duplicate NaN handling code (now in pyAnyEql)

### runtime.zig
- Update pyTupleEql to use pyAnyEql for field comparison
- Update containsGeneric to use pyAnyEql for element matching
- Update dict value comparison to use pyAnyEql

## Benefits
- Single source of truth for Python equality semantics
- Consistent NaN identity handling across all comparison paths
- Simplified code by removing duplicate logic

Tests passing: int_literal, unary, longexp, binop, bool, float (6 tests)
